title: HHAV TRANSITIONAL HOUSING PROGRAM - 211info
text: HELPING HANDS AGAINST VIOLENCE HHAV TRANSITIONAL HOUSING PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides year-long transitional housing with medical and legal advocacy, including accompaniment to medical, legal, and court appointments; assistance filling out medical and legal forms; transportation, food, clothing, peer counseling, and peer support. Eligibility: Survivors and their children who are victims of sexual assault, domestic violence, or stalking Hours: Office: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Crisis Line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 386-6603 247 Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (541) 386-6603 Hotline 24/7 (541) 386-4808 Main phone Hotline (541) 386-6603 24/7 Main phone (541) 386-4808 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Hood River County Services: Crime Victim Accompaniment Services Crime Victim Support Groups General Crime Victim Assistance Transitional Housing/Shelter * Crime Victims Other Services Offered At This Location: HOUSING/SHELTER - HOTLINE AND EMERGENCY SERVICES If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. HELPING HANDS AGAINST VIOLENCE
